title: CAIRN — Clinical interoperability reference architecture. Built on FM-2.
description:
CAIRN is an open-source reference architecture for formal verification of clinical interoperability mappings. It implements FM-2 — a mathematical framework modelling clinical data through a type system as a directed acyclic graph (DAG), Allen temporal algebra (13 interval relations), and a universal event model as a 6-tuple.

CAIRN includes SILD (Silent Information Loss Detector), which formally verifies CDR-to-FHIR mappings and classifies detected losses as REGRESSION, IMPROVEMENT, PERSISTENT, SILENT_LOSS, or DRIFT using Z3 SMT proofs.

Version 1.0.1 closes 9 FM-2 compliance gaps: full DRIFT classification, IMPROVEMENT detection across mapping versions, multi-event code handling, extended terminology drift coverage (8 system-pair mappings), CLI/API tests, and corrected Allen algebra documentation. 50 tests pass.

Published on PyPI as cairn-clinical (pip install cairn-clinical).

NOT a medical device as defined by EU MDR 2017/745, EU IVDR 2017/746, or MPDG. For research and interoperability validation only.
references: Allen, J.F. (1983). Maintaining knowledge about temporal intervals. Communications of the ACM, 26(11), 832-843.
openEHR Foundation. openEHR Architecture Overview. https://specifications.openehr.org/
ISO 23903:2021 Health informatics — Interoperability and integration reference architecture.
de Moura, L., Bjorner, N. (2008). Z3: An Efficient SMT Solver. TACAS 2008.
notes: CAIRN is NOT a medical device as defined by EU MDR 2017/745, EU IVDR 2017/746, or MPDG (Deutschland). It is intended exclusively for research, education, and interoperability system validation.
